div.p: The North Atlantic Council, at the conclusion of its seventh session held in Ottawa, Canada, September 15–20, 1951, agreed to the inclusion of Greece and Turkey in the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
For documentation regarding the inclusion of Greece and Turkey, see volume III, part 1, pages 460 ff. and 616 ff.
The Protocol to the North Atlantic Treaty admitting Greece and Turkey to membership in the Organization was signed on October 17, 1951. It is printed in Documents (R.I.I.A.) for 1951, page 65.
